summary: PR 2633 split Kirby SpecialHi code out of the previous ftkirbyspecialn range into a new matched object, adding ftkirbyspecialhi.c/.h, updating GALE01 split ranges and configure.py, moving SpecialHi prototypes out of ftkirby.h, and wiring the new header include into ftkirby.c. The code slice shows several explicit matching-only tactics such as PAD_STACK, padding locals, a fake permuter match comment, and a comma-operator regalloc comment.

key_files.role: Defines the new ftkirbyspecialhi.c text/sdata2 ownership range and narrows the ftkirbyspecialn.c range.
Adds melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialhi.c as a Matching object.
New 552-line source file containing the split Kirby SpecialHi functions from 0x800F21E8 through before 0x800F331C.
New header for the split SpecialHi declarations.
Removes SpecialHi-related prototypes from the aggregate Kirby header after the split.
Receives large edits as SpecialHi code is split away from the previous SpecialN translation unit range.
Large deletion plus one addition in fighter data, likely related to linking the newly split Kirby object, but exact semantic details are not visible in the loaded excerpt.
Adds Kirby inline support used by the split source, exact details not visible in the loaded excerpt.
Small cleanup connected to the split, exact details not visible in the loaded excerpt.

what_changed: Created src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialhi.c with Kirby SpecialHi, SpecialAirHi, animation, IASA, physics, and collision handlers.
Created src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialhi.h and removed the corresponding SpecialHi prototypes from ftkirby.h.
Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt so ftkirbyspecialhi.c owns .text 0x800F21E8-0x800F331C and .sdata2 0x804D9370-0x804D9388, while ftkirbyspecialn.c starts at 0x800F331C and .sdata2 at 0x804D9388.
Added ftkirbyspecialhi.c to configure.py as Object(Matching, "melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialhi.c").

The PR also changed ftkirbyspecialn.c, ftdata.c, ftkirbyyoshiegg.c, and ftKirby/inlines.h, but the loaded excerpt does not show enough detail to summarize their exact semantic edits beyond the split/link context.
smart_moves: Kept the split metadata and build configuration in sync: splits.txt was updated at the same time configure.py marked the new object Matching.
Moved declarations toward a feature-specific header instead of leaving all SpecialHi prototypes in the broader ftkirby.h header.
Preserved an address-style static helper name, fn_800F21E8, where the loaded evidence does not prove a semantic name.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When carving a matched function range out of a larger translation unit, update split ranges, build object classification, source/header placement, and includes together so the new unit can link independently.
A focused split can leave surrounding nonmatching code in the older file while marking only the newly separated object as Matching.

naming_conventions.convention: Kirby SpecialHi handlers use the ftKb_SpecialHi*/ftKb_SpecialAirHi* prefix with phase suffixes such as Enter, Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll.
Unknown or not-yet-semantic local helper names may remain address-style, as shown by static fn_800F21E8.
File naming follows the lowercase character/action pattern: ftkirbyspecialhi.c and ftkirbyspecialhi.h.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Uses PAD_STACK in multiple functions, including ftKb_SpecialHi4_Anim, ftKb_SpecialAirHiEnd_Anim, ftKb_SpecialHi1_Phys, ftKb_SpecialHi2_Phys, ftKb_SpecialHi2_Coll, and ftKb_SpecialAirHi2_Coll.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Loaded standards say PAD_STACK requires narrow verifier evidence before treating it as reviewable source; the PR slice shows the tactic but does not include objdiff details.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Uses explicit fake-match comments and permuter-shaped code, including an empty condition marked '// fake match, via permuter'.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This is evidence of a matching workaround, not a general style recommendation.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Uses stack/padding locals such as u8 _pad[12] and f32 stack comments to influence stack shape.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Loaded standards require explicit build/objdiff evidence for dummy locals or widened stack lifetimes.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Uses a comma operator for register allocation in ftKb_SpecialAirHi3_Phys, with the inline comment 'FAKE MATCH: comma operator required for regalloc'.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Treat as a narrow matching tactic tied to this function unless later evidence supports a cleaner source form.

review_feedback.text: The loaded PR slice reports zero review comments and zero reviews.

follow_up_queries: ftkirbyspecialhi PAD_STACK fake match permuter regalloc

The diff excerpt is truncated, so exact contents of ftkirbyspecialhi.h, ftdata.c, ftkirbyspecialn.c, ftkirbyyoshiegg.c, and inlines.h are not fully visible.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Split matched subrange with coordinated split/build/header updates
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2633 shows a matched Kirby SpecialHi range being split into its own source/header while also updating GALE01 split ownership, configure.py Matching object registration, and includes/prototypes.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep unsupported helper names address-style during split
accepted_candidate_records.text: The new ftkirbyspecialhi.c keeps fn_800F21E8 as an address-style static helper rather than inventing a semantic name in the loaded evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: ftkirbyspecialhi split ownership in PR 2633
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2633 adds melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialhi.c with .text start 0x800F21E8 end 0x800F331C and .sdata2 start 0x804D9370 end 0x804D9388; ftkirbyspecialn.c is narrowed to start at .text 0x800F331C and .sdata2 0x804D9388.
source_update_candidates.reason: Concrete split-range data is source-owned path/split information and should be curated as a path fact if still useful.

source_update_candidates.title: Require verifier notes for fake-match comments in linked matching objects
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2633 contains explicit fake-match comments, PAD_STACK uses, padding locals, and a comma-operator regalloc workaround in a file registered as Matching. Consider whether standards should explicitly ask authors to leave a narrow verifier note or cleanup TODO for such tactics when linking a new matched object.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded global standards already cover this area, but this PR provides a concrete historical example for curator review rather than an automatically accepted new rule.

rejection_notes: Do not treat the visible fake-match, PAD_STACK, dummy local, or comma-operator patterns as accepted style; they are only evidence of tactics used in this PR and require verifier context under loaded standards.
Do not infer semantic meaning for fn_800F21E8 beyond its visible use as an accessory4_cb helper that can spawn an item/effect path; the PR evidence does not provide a confirmed name.
Do not summarize the ftdata.c, ftkirbyspecialn.c, ftkirbyyoshiegg.c, or inlines.h semantic edits beyond split/link context because their detailed diff hunks are not loaded.
